layout: post
title:  "BigQuery and BQML: A Powerful Combination for Data Analysis"
date:   2023-08-29 06:30:00 +0000
categories: gcp vertex-ai gcloud workbench bigquery bq bqml
author: Ali Cabukel
---

*Content was generated by Bard*

BigQuery is a fully managed, cloud-based data warehouse that enables businesses to analyze all their data very quickly. It is a powerful tool for storing and analyzing large amounts of data. However, it can be challenging to use BigQuery effectively without the right tools.

This is where BQML comes in. BQML is a powerful query language that is specifically designed for BigQuery. It allows users to easily query and analyze data in BigQuery. With BQML, users can quickly and easily get the insights they need from their data.

One of the biggest advantages of using BQML is its simplicity. BQML is a very easy-to-learn language, even for those who are not familiar with SQL. This makes it a great option for businesses that want to get started with BigQuery quickly and easily.

Another advantage of BQML is its power. BQML is a very powerful language that can be used to perform complex queries and analysis. This makes it a great option for businesses that need to get deep insights from their data.

Overall, BigQuery and BQML are a powerful combination for data analysis. BigQuery provides the storage and processing power, while BQML provides the easy-to-use query language. Together, they make it easy for businesses to get the insights they need from their data.

Here are some of the benefits of using BigQuery and BQML:

* **Speed:** BigQuery is a very fast data warehouse. It can query and analyze large amounts of data in seconds. This makes it a great option for businesses that need to get insights from their data quickly.
* **Scalability:** BigQuery is a scalable data warehouse. It can handle large amounts of data, and it can scale up or down as needed. This makes it a great option for businesses that need to analyze large amounts of data.
* **Cost-effectiveness:** BigQuery is a cost-effective data warehouse. It is priced based on the amount of data that is stored and processed. This makes it a great option for businesses that want to get the most out of their data without spending a lot of money.

If you are looking for a powerful and scalable data warehouse, then BigQuery is a great option. And if you want to get the most out of BigQuery, then BQML is a great choice.

### Building ML pipelines using BMQL

1. Pre-requisites
2. Creating a Workbench user-managed notebook
3. Getting the data
4. Developing BQML queries


#### Pre-requisites

- Acccess to GCP with a proper billing account along with VertexAI, Cloud Storage and BigQuery services
- Service Accounts and Network settings must have been configured properly
- Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud` and `gsutil`) must have been installed and configured
- The following environment variables must have been set

```shell
BUCKET_NAME=<Bucket Name>
REGION=<Region>
ZONE=<Zone>
PROJECT_ID=<GCP Project ID>
SERVICE_ACCOUNT=<Service Account>
SUBNETWORK=<Subnetwork Name>
LOCATION=<BigQuery Dataset Location>
```

#### Creating a Workbench user-managed notebook

The following script will create a workbench user-managed notebook `telco-churn-tf-notebook` along with the following options:
- `n1-standard-4` machine type
- `tf2-ent-2-6-cu110-notebooks-v20211202-debian-10` image name from `deeplearning-platform-release` image project

```shell
gcloud notebooks instances create telco-churn-tf-notebook \
        --project $PROJECT_ID \
        --location $ZONE \
        --vm-image-project=deeplearning-platform-release \
        --vm-image-name=tf2-ent-2-6-cu110-notebooks-v20211202-debian-10 \
        --machine-type=n1-standard-4 \
        --service-account=$SERVICE_ACCOUNT \
        --subnet=$SUBNETWORK \
        --subnet-region=$REGION \
        --metadata=report-system-health=TRUE 
```

#### Getting the data

- The following command can be executed on workbench notebook in order to upload data into a bucket.

```shell
gsutil cp train.csv gs://$BUCKET_NAME/telco_churn/data/train.csv
gsutil cp valid.csv gs://$BUCKET_NAME/telco_churn/data/valid.csv
gsutil cp test.csv gs://$BUCKET_NAME/telco_churn/data/test.csv
```

- `bq` tool enables us to create a new dataset `telco_churn_db` on BigQuery

```shell
bq --location=$LOCATION --project_id=$PROJECT_ID mk -d telco_churn_db
```

- `schema.json` defines the BigQuery table's structure.

```json
[
  {
    "description": "MonthlyCharges",
    "mode": "NULLABLE",
    "name": "MonthlyCharges",
    "type": "FLOAT"
  },
  {
    "description": "TotalCharges",
    "mode": "NULLABLE",
    "name": "TotalCharges",
    "type": "FLOAT"
  },
  {
    "description": "tenure",
    "mode": "NULLABLE",
    "name": "tenure",
    "type": "INTEGER"
  },
  {
    "description": "gender",
    "mode": "NULLABLE",
    "name": "gender",
    "type": "STRING"
  },
  {
    "description": "SeniorCitizen",
    "mode": "NULLABLE",
    "name": "SeniorCitizen",
    "type": "STRING"
  },
  {
    "description": "Partner",
    "mode": "NULLABLE",
    "name": "Partner",
    "type": "STRING"
  },
  {
    "description": "Dependents",
    "mode": "NULLABLE",
    "name": "Dependents",
    "type": "STRING"
  },
  {
    "description": "PhoneService",
    "mode": "NULLABLE",
    "name": "PhoneService",
    "type": "STRING"
  },
  {
    "description": "MultipleLines",
    "mode": "NULLABLE",
    "name": "MultipleLines",
    "type": "STRING"
  },
  {
    "description": "InternetService",
    "mode": "NULLABLE",
    "name": "InternetService",
    "type": "STRING"
  },
  {
    "description": "OnlineSecurity",
    "mode": "NULLABLE",
    "name": "OnlineSecurity",
    "type": "STRING"
  },
  {
    "description": "OnlineBackup",
    "mode": "NULLABLE",
    "name": "OnlineBackup",
    "type": "STRING"
  },
  {
    "description": "DeviceProtection",
    "mode": "NULLABLE",
    "name": "DeviceProtection",
    "type": "STRING"
  },
  {
    "description": "TechSupport",
    "mode": "NULLABLE",
    "name": "TechSupport",
    "type": "STRING"
  },
  {
    "description": "StreamingTV",
    "mode": "NULLABLE",
    "name": "StreamingTV",
    "type": "STRING"
  },
  {
    "description": "StreamingMovies",
    "mode": "NULLABLE",
    "name": "StreamingMovies",
    "type": "STRING"
  },
  {
    "description": "Contract",
    "mode": "NULLABLE",
    "name": "Contract",
    "type": "STRING"
  },
  {
    "description": "PaperlessBilling",
    "mode": "NULLABLE",
    "name": "PaperlessBilling",
    "type": "STRING"
  },
  {
    "description": "PaymentMethod",
    "mode": "NULLABLE",
    "name": "PaymentMethod",
    "type": "STRING"
  },
  {
    "description": "Churn",
    "mode": "NULLABLE",
    "name": "Churn",
    "type": "INTEGER"
  }
]
```

- Creating `train` table under `telco_churn_db` dataset with the given `schema.json` schema and `train.csv` data source

```shell
bq --location=$LOCATION --project_id=$PROJECT_ID load \
    telco_churn_db.train \
    gs://$BUCKET_NAME/telco_churn/data/train.csv \
    schema.json
```

- Creating `valid` table under `telco_churn_db` dataset with the given `schema.json` schema and `valid.csv` data source

```shell
bq --location=$LOCATION --project_id=$PROJECT_ID load \
    telco_churn_db.valid \
    gs://$BUCKET_NAME/telco_churn/data/valid.csv \
    schema.json
```

- Creating `test` table under `telco_churn_db` dataset with the given `schema.json` schema and `test.csv` data source

```shell
bq --location=$LOCATION --project_id=$PROJECT_ID load \
    telco_churn_db.test \
    gs://$BUCKET_NAME/telco_churn/data/test.csv \
    schema.json
```

#### Developing BQML queries

- Buiilding a base line `xgboost model`
    + Model name: `telco_churn_db.baseline_xgb`
    + Target variable: `Churn`
    + Global explain is true
    + Training data: `telco_churn_db.train`

```sql
%%bigquery
CREATE OR REPLACE MODEL telco_churn_db.baseline_xgb
OPTIONS(
    model_type='BOOSTED_TREE_CLASSIFIER', 
    input_label_cols=['Churn'],
    enable_global_explain=TRUE
) AS
SELECT  t.* FROM telco_churn_db.train t
```

- Evaluating the base line model
    + Evaluate the model against the validataion and test data
    + Confusition matrix and ROC curve against the test data

```sql
%%bigquery
SELECT * FROM ML.EVALUATE(MODEL telco_churn_db.baseline_xgb, TABLE telco_churn_db.valid)
```

```sql
%%bigquery
SELECT * FROM ML.EVALUATE(MODEL telco_churn_db.baseline_xgb, TABLE telco_churn_db.test)
```

```sql
%%bigquery
SELECT * FROM ML.CONFUSION_MATRIX(MODEL telco_churn_db.baseline_xgb, TABLE telco_churn_db.test)
```

```sql
%%bigquery
SELECT * FROM ML.ROC_CURVE(MODEL telco_churn_db.baseline_xgb, TABLE telco_churn_db.test)
```

- Getting the training information of the base line model

```sql
%%bigquery
SELECT * FROM ML.TRAINING_INFO(MODEL telco_churn_db.baseline_xgb)
ORDER BY iteration
```

- Getting the feature information (descriptive stats) of the baseline model

```sql
%%bigquery
SELECT * FROM ML.FEATURE_INFO(MODEL telco_churn_db.baseline_xgb)
```

- Explaining the base line model globally

```sql
%%bigquery
SELECT * FROM ML.GLOBAL_EXPLAIN(MODEL telco_churn_db.baseline_xgb)
```

- Getting the feature importance of the base line model

```sql
%%bigquery
SELECT * FROM ML.FEATURE_IMPORTANCE(MODEL telco_churn_db.baseline_xgb)
```

- Predicting the base line model against the test data

```sql
%%bigquery
SELECT  predicted_Churn,
        predicted_Churn_probs,
        Churn
FROM ML.PREDICT (MODEL telco_churn_db.baseline_xgb, TABLE telco_churn_db.test)
LIMIT 10
```

- Local explanations of the base line model against the test data

```sql
%%bigquery
WITH explain AS(
SELECT 
        predicted_Churn,
        probability,
        top_feature_attributions,
        baseline_prediction_value,
        prediction_value,
        approximation_error,
        Churn
FROM ML.EXPLAIN_PREDICT
    (
        MODEL telco_churn_db.baseline_xgb,
        TABLE telco_churn_db.test,
        STRUCT(3 as top_k_features)
    )
LIMIT 10
)
SELECT * EXCEPT(top_feature_attributions)
    FROM explain CROSS JOIN UNNEST(explain.top_feature_attributions) AS attributions
```

- Advanced: Hyper parameter tuning 
    + Model name: `telco_churn_db.hpt_xgb`
    + Data source: `telco_churn_db.train`
    + Label: `Churn`
    + Model type: `BOOSTED_TREE_CLASSIFIER`
    + Number of trials: `20`, Tuning Algo: `VIZIER_DEFAULT`, Tuning Objective: `ROC_AUC`
    + Grid: `booster_type`=[dart, gbtree], `learn_rate`=(0, 0.9), `max_tree_depth`=(1, 20)

```sql
%%bigquery
CREATE OR REPLACE MODEL telco_churn_db.hpt_xgb
OPTIONS(model_type='BOOSTED_TREE_CLASSIFIER',
        input_label_cols=['Churn'],
        enable_global_explain=TRUE,
        NUM_TRIALS=20,
        HPARAM_TUNING_ALGORITHM='VIZIER_DEFAULT',
        HPARAM_TUNING_OBJECTIVES=['ROC_AUC'],
        booster_type=hparam_candidates(['dart', 'gbtree']),
        learn_rate=hparam_range(0, 0.9),
        max_tree_depth=hparam_range(1, 20)
       )
AS
SELECT  t.*
FROM telco_churn_db.train t
```

- Advanced: Getting the trial info of Hyper parameter tuning

```sql
%%bigquery
SELECT  hyperparameters.learn_rate, 
        hyperparameters.max_tree_depth,
        hyperparameters.booster_type,
        hparam_tuning_evaluation_metrics.roc_auc,
        *  
FROM  ML.TRIAL_INFO(MODEL staging.hpt_xgb)
WHERE status = 'SUCCEEDED'
ORDER BY eval_loss
```

- Advanced: Transform statement
    + BQML handles feature pre-processing automatically
    + Also, feature pre-processing can be done manually
    + `TRANSFORM` statements allows us to define featue pre-processing as a part of model creation
    + At prediction time, there is no need to apply manual pre-processing. Prediction handles raw data, applies pre-processing automatically before scoring

```sql
%%bigquery
CREATE OR REPLACE MODEL telco_churn_db.churn_final_model
TRANSFORM(
         Churn,
         ML.BUCKETIZE(ML.MIN_MAX_SCALER(MonthlyCharges) OVER() , GENERATE_ARRAY(0, 1, 0.1)) AS MonthlyCharges_Bin,
         ML.BUCKETIZE(ML.MIN_MAX_SCALER(TotalCharges) OVER() , GENERATE_ARRAY(0, 1, 0.1)) AS TotalCharges_Bin,
         ML.QUANTILE_BUCKETIZE(tenure, 5) OVER() AS tenure_tiles,
         ML.FEATURE_CROSS(STRUCT(Partner, Dependents)) AS PartnerDependent_Fc,
         ML.FEATURE_CROSS(STRUCT(PhoneService, MultipleLines)) AS PhoneMulti_Fc,
         ML.FEATURE_CROSS(STRUCT(InternetService, OnlineSecurity)) AS InternetOnlineSecurity_Fc,
         ML.FEATURE_CROSS(STRUCT(InternetService, OnlineBackup)) AS InternetOnlineBackup_Fc,
         ML.FEATURE_CROSS(STRUCT(DeviceProtection, OnlineBackup)) AS DeviceOnlineBackup_Fc,
         ML.FEATURE_CROSS(STRUCT(DeviceProtection, OnlineSecurity)) AS DeviceOnlineSecurity_Fc,
         ML.FEATURE_CROSS(STRUCT(StreamingTV, StreamingMovies)) AS Streaming_Fc,
         ML.FEATURE_CROSS(STRUCT(PaperlessBilling, PaymentMethod)) AS Payment_Fc
    )
    OPTIONS(
        input_label_cols=['Churn'],
        model_type='BOOSTED_TREE_CLASSIFIER',
        enable_global_explain=TRUE,
        learn_rate=0.9,
        max_tree_depth=2,
        booster_type='dart'
    ) AS
SELECT *
FROM telco_churn_db.train  t
```
